description_long: This is a vintage-style fortune teller kiosk, reminiscent of the iconic Zoltar machines found in amusement parks and boardwalks. The front features a glass display with a mechanical fortune teller figure inside, hands poised over a crystal ball, and the bold red 'ZOLTAR' sign above. The lower section has a coin slot and a rectangular panel, likely for dispensing fortune cards. The structure is primarily constructed from wood with ornate metallic trim framing the front and sides. The sides and back are solid wood panels, while the top is a slightly slanted wooden roof. The kiosk stands on four curved wooden legs, giving it a classic, old-fashioned appearance.
description: A vintage wooden Zoltar fortune teller kiosk with ornate trim, glass display, and coin slot, standing on curved legs.
description_view_0: Front view showing the Zoltar sign, glass display with fortune teller figure, coin slot, and ornate trim.
description_view_1: Left side view revealing the side of the fortune teller figure through the glass and the ornate frame.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a plain wooden panel with no visible features or openings.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the fortune teller figure through the glass and the ornate frame, similar to the left side.
attribute_signage_type: Printed sign integrated into the structure above the glass, displaying 'ZOLTAR' in bold red letters.
attribute_window_presence: One large rectangular glass window on the front, none on sides or rear.
attribute_roof_design: Flat, slightly slanted wooden roof with no overhang or decorative elements.
attribute_architectural_style: Vintage amusement, with ornate trim and classic arcade influences.
